title: VimFu — Ctrl-B
description: Ctrl-B scrolls one full page backward (up). Same as Page Up. Use it to read through long files quickly.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Quest Log

hero = "Aria"
level = 12
hp = 85
mp = 40
xp = 0
gold = 100

def enter_forest():
    print("Shadows close in")
    enemies = ["wolf", "bat", "troll"]
    for e in enemies:
        print(f"A wild {e} appears!")
    print("Forest cleared")

def enter_cave():
    print("Dripping water echoes")
    traps = ["spike pit", "falling rocks"]
    for trap in traps:
        print(f"Watch out: {trap}!")
    print("Cave explored")

def find_treasure():
    gold = 500
    gems = ["ruby", "emerald", "diamond"]
    print(f"Found {gold} gold")
    for g in gems:
        print(f"Found a {g}!")
    print("Inventory full")

def boss_fight():
    boss_hp = 200
    boss_name = "Dragon King"
    print(f"{boss_name} attacks!")
    while boss_hp > 0:
        boss_hp -= 25
        print(f"{boss_name}: {boss_hp} HP")
    print("Victory!")

def level_up(hero):
    hero["level"] += 1
    hero["hp"] += 20
    hero["mp"] += 10
    print(f"Level {hero['level']}!")

def save_game():
    data = {"hero": hero, "level": level}
    print("Game saved.")
    return True

def load_game():
    print("Loading save...")
    return {"hero": "Aria", "level": 12}

setup.comment: Launch nvim and jump to bottom so we can scroll up

steps.say: Control B scrolls one full page backward. That means up.
We're at the bottom of this file. Press control B.
One full page up. The screen jumped to show earlier code.
Near the top now. Two presses scrolled through most of the file.
Control F does the opposite. It pages forward, meaning down.
Back in the middle. B for backward, F for forward.
One gotcha. If you use T mux, control B is the T mux prefix key. T mux intercepts it before Vim sees it. Most people remap the T mux prefix to control space to fix that.
Control B. Page up. A full screen at a time.
